title: Deezer Reveals: Over 50% of Daily Uploads on the Platform Are AI-Generated Music, Content Not Played for 6 Months Will Be Cleaned Up
excerpt: Music streaming platform Deezer recently released shocking data: AI-generated music now accounts for more than 50% of its daily uploads. In June this year, the
content_html:
Music streaming platform Deezer recently released shocking data: AI-generated music now accounts for more than 50% of its daily uploads. In June this year, the platform saw a peak in daily AI music uploads, with an average of about 90,000 tracks per day.

The rapid influx of AI music is forcing major streaming platforms to redefine content boundaries, but the industry has yet to reach a consensus. Bandcamp has directly banned AI-generated tracks, while Tidal cut off monetization channels for related content; Apple Music uses a voluntary AI tagging system, and Spotify has established its own AI usage ratio policy. Deezer's latest response is to remove AI-generated tracks that have not been played in the past six months, as well as content involving view-fraud to obtain revenue.

Deezer CEO Alexis Lanternier stated in a statement, "Deezer has been at the forefront of fighting fraud and reducing the dilution of AI music revenue for the past two years. Now, half of the daily uploads are AI-generated tracks, and we are taking additional measures to protect the rights of artists and songwriters, while focusing on the music that fans truly love."

Looking back at Deezer's tracking data, the expansion of AI music has been continuously accelerating. In January 2025, daily AI-generated track uploads were about 10,000, accounting for 10% of the total daily uploads; by April of the same year, it increased to 20,000 (18%), and by September reached 30,000 (28%), rising to 50,000 (34%) in November. Entering 2026, January had 60,000 (39%), April reached 75,000 (44%), and peaked at 90,000 in June.

Since last year, Deezer has added tags for AI music on its platform and stated that its detection technology can identify tracks generated by AI music startups like Suno and Udio—two companies currently facing copyright lawsuits. At the beginning of this year, Deezer opened its detection technology to other platforms, but it remains unclear whether any mainstream platforms have integrated the tool. Last month, Deezer also launched a tool to screen AI-generated tracks in playlists on Apple Music and Spotify.

content_html_zh:
音乐流媒体平台 Deezer 最近发布了令人震惊的数据：人工智能生成的音乐目前占其每日上传量的 50% 以上。 今年6月，该平台的AI音乐日上传量达到峰值，平均每天约9万首曲目。

AI音乐的快速涌入正迫使各大流媒体平台重新定义内容边界，但业界尚未达成共识。 Bandcamp直接封杀了AI生成的曲目，Tidal则切断了相关内容的变现渠道； Apple Music 使用自愿的 AI 标记系统，Spotify 也制定了自己的 AI 使用比例政策。 Deezer 的最新回应是删除人工智能生成的过去六个月内未播放的曲目，以及涉及观看欺诈以获取收入的内容。

Deezer 首席执行官 Alexis Lanternier 在一份声明中表示，“过去两年来，Deezer 一直处于打击欺诈和减少人工智能音乐收入稀释的最前沿。 现在，每日上传的曲目中有一半是人工智能生成的曲目，我们正在采取额外措施保护艺术家和词曲作者的权利，同时专注于粉丝真正喜爱的音乐。”

回顾Deezer的追踪数据，AI音乐的扩张一直在不断加速。 2025年1月，每日AI生成曲目上传量约为1万条，占每日上传总量的10%；到同年4月，增加到20,000人（18%），到9月达到30,000人（28%），11月增加到50,000人（34%）。 进入2026年，1月有6万（39%），4月达到7.5万（44%），6月达到峰值9万。

自去年以来，Deezer 在其平台上添加了人工智能音乐标签，并表示其检测技术可以识别由 Suno 和 Udio 等人工智能音乐初创公司生成的曲目，这两家公司目前正面临版权诉讼。 今年年初，Deezer向其他平台开放了其检测技术，但目前尚不清楚是否有主流平台集成了该工具。 上个月，Deezer 还推出了一款工具，可以在 Apple Music 和 Spotify 的播放列表中筛选人工智能生成的曲目。
